prompt: The source PRD already lives in an existing GitHub parent issue whose body and comments are already in context. Keep the parent issue's metadata untouched, but update its body with tracking guidance instead of creating a separate tracker issue. Parent issue summary: saved searches can be created from the search page, alerts can email users when results change, and users need a way to pause alerts without deleting the saved search. Existing issue comments add two constraints: (1) shipping saved-search CRUD before alert delivery is acceptable, and (2) notification preference edits should not block initial saved-search creation. Turn this into independently-grabbable GitHub issues. Because the source is an existing GitHub issue, present the vertical-slice breakdown first, then draft the child issues and managed parent-body tracking guidance that makes clear the executable work remains in the parent issue's sibling direct sub-issues and which AFK task is next.
assertions: Presents a numbered proposed breakdown before creating child issues because the source is an existing GitHub issue.
The proposed breakdown includes execution-wave or equivalent ready-order guidance so future agents can tell what comes next.

Updates the existing parent issue body with a managed task-graph/how-to-grab-work section instead of creating a queue-guide child issue, and makes clear that the executable tasks remain sibling direct sub-issues of the PRD issue.
Queue guidance on the parent tells future agents to pick the lowest ready AFK wave/task whose blockers are closed, and to hold HITL work until the named decision or review is complete.
Uses the issue comments to keep saved-search creation earlier than alert delivery and to avoid blocking initial creation on notification preference editing.
Attaches child issues to the parent using addSubIssue without GraphQL variables in the mutation signature.
